html:
Anders (originally Andreas) Dahl, born March 17, 1751, in Varnhem, Skaraborg County, and died May 25, 1789, was a Swedish botanist. He was a half-brother to Olof Kolmodin Jr., and a full brother to Christopher Dahl and Erik Dahl. At the age of 4, he moved to Saleby.

He enrolled at Uppsala University on April 3, 1770, where he became a student of Carl von Linné. He also worked to preserve Linné's extensive herbarium. During his studies, Dahl was employed for several years by Clas Alströmer, overseeing his garden and collections. In his youth, he was a member of the Swedish Topographic Society, founded in Skara in 1769, which conducted natural history studies.

In 1786, Dahl obtained a medical doctorate from Kiel. He became a botanical demonstrator and a medical lecturer in Åbo in 1787. He published "Observationes botanicæ cirka Systema vegetabilium divi a Linné editum" (1787). After Linné's death, Dahl attempted to secure his collections for Sweden and offered to purchase them for the same amount as J. E. Smith, but was unsuccessful. The Spanish botanist Antonio José Cavanilles named the *Dahlia* genus after Anders Dahl. The author's name "Dahl" can be used to refer to Anders Dahl in connection with a scientific name in botany; see Wikipedia articles that link to the author's name.

== Sources == Svensk uppslagsbok. Malmö 1931.

== Further Reading == Birger, S.: Anders Dahl in Svenskt biografiskt lexikon (1931)

== External Links == Dahl, 2. Anders in Nordisk familjebok (second edition, 1906) Dahl, 2. Anders in Herman Hofberg, Svenskt biografiskt handlexikon (second edition, 1906) The Swedish National Museum of Natural History

How certain are we about this?

Information about figures from the Age of Liberty is well-documented through parliamentary records, letters, and archives. However, interpretations of their roles are often influenced by later political sympathies.
tldr: Anders Dahl was a Swedish botanist who studied plants and became a student of the renowned scientist Carl Linnaeus. He is so significant in his field that the flower *dahlia* is named after him.
lattlast:
Anders Dahl was born in 1751 and became an expert in plants, a field known as botany. During his studies at the University of Uppsala, he worked closely with Carl von Linné, who was a very famous researcher. He helped to care for gardens and collections of plants.

Later, he earned a doctorate in medicine and obtained important positions in science. He also wrote books about plants and co-founded an organization dedicated to studying nature. He worked extensively to preserve old plant collections so that they would not be lost.

Because he did so much important work in his field, another researcher named the flower *dahlia* after him. Today, his name is used in scientific texts when discussing plants. His life and work are well-documented in historical books.
